---
title: "brms"
author: "Marta"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}
df <- read.csv("smartwatch_minable_view.csv")

minable_view <- df %>%filter(!is.na(HR_GoldStandard)) %>%filter(HR_GoldStandard >= 30 & HR_GoldStandard <= 200)
print(paste("Máximo BPM en ECG tras filtrar:", max(minable_view$HR_GoldStandard)))
```


# INLA

```{r}
library(INLA)
library(dplyr)

df <- read.csv("smartwatch_minable_view.csv")

CAD_val <- 5  # Clinical Allowed Difference (BPM)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# FUNCIÓN PRINCIPAL: modelo INLA sobre diff
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
fit_inla_diff <- function(df_sub) {
  df_sub <- df_sub %>%
    mutate(
      diff   = as.numeric(HR_Device) - as.numeric(HR_GoldStandard),
      ID_idx = as.numeric(as.factor(ID))
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(diff))
  
  if (nrow(df_sub) < 30) return(NULL)
  
  fit <- inla(diff ~ 1 + f(ID_idx, model = "iid"),
              data = df_sub,
              family = "gaussian",
              control.compute = list(config = TRUE, dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE),
              control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE))
  
  # Parámetros del posterior
  alpha     <- fit$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "mean"]
  alpha_lo  <- fit$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "0.025quant"]
  alpha_hi  <- fit$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "0.975quant"]
  
  prec_e    <- fit$summary.hyperpar["Precision for the Gaussian observations", "mean"]
  prec_id   <- fit$summary.hyperpar["Precision for ID_idx", "mean"]
  
  sigma_e   <- 1 / sqrt(prec_e)
  sigma_id  <- 1 / sqrt(prec_id)
  sigma_tot <- sqrt(sigma_e^2 + sigma_id^2)
  
  # Muestras del posterior para TDI y CP (esto es lo bayesiano de verdad)
  n_sim      <- 20000
  alpha_sim  <- rnorm(n_sim, alpha, sigma_tot)
  
  # CCC real: 2*cov(x,y) / (var(x) + var(y) + (mean(x)-mean(y))^2)
  x <- as.numeric(df_sub$HR_GoldStandard)
  y <- as.numeric(df_sub$HR_Device)
  ccc_val <- (2 * cov(x, y)) / (var(x) + var(y) + (mean(x) - mean(y))^2)
  
  data.frame(
    alpha         = round(alpha, 3),        # Sesgo medio (BPM)
    alpha_CI_low  = round(alpha_lo, 3),     # IC 95% inferior
    alpha_CI_high = round(alpha_hi, 3),     # IC 95% superior
    sigma_patient = round(sigma_id, 3),     # SD variabilidad entre sujetos
    sigma_residual= round(sigma_e, 3),      # SD error residual
    LoA_Inf       = round(alpha - 1.96 * sigma_tot, 3),
    LoA_Sup       = round(alpha + 1.96 * sigma_tot, 3),
    TDI_95        = round(quantile(abs(alpha_sim), 0.95), 3),
    CP            = round(mean(abs(alpha_sim) <= CAD_val), 4),
    CIA           = round(sigma_id^2 / (sigma_id^2 + sigma_e^2), 4),
    CCC           = round(ccc_val, 4),
    DIC           = round(fit$dic$dic, 2),
    WAIC          = round(fit$waic$waic, 2)
  )
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. TABLA GLOBAL (un modelo por dispositivo)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
dispositivos <- unique(df$Device)

tabla_global <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dispositivos, function(dev) {
  df_sub <- df %>% filter(Device == dev,
                          !is.na(HR_GoldStandard),
                          !is.na(HR_Device),
                          HR_GoldStandard >= 30,
                          HR_GoldStandard <= 200)
  res <- fit_inla_diff(df_sub)
  if (!is.null(res)) cbind(Device = dev, res)
}))

cat("\n===== TABLA GLOBAL =====\n")
print(tabla_global)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. TABLA POR ACTIVIDAD
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
actividades <- unique(df$Activity)

tabla_activity <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dispositivos, function(dev) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(actividades, function(act) {
    df_sub <- df %>% filter(Device == dev, Activity == act,
                            !is.na(HR_GoldStandard),
                            !is.na(HR_Device),
                            HR_GoldStandard >= 30,
                            HR_GoldStandard <= 200)
    res <- fit_inla_diff(df_sub)
    if (!is.null(res)) cbind(Device = dev, Activity = act, res)
  }))
}))

cat("\n===== TABLA POR ACTIVIDAD =====\n")
print(tabla_activity)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. TABLA POR SKIN TONE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
skin_tones <- unique(df$Skin.Tone)

tabla_skin <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dispositivos, function(dev) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(skin_tones, function(st) {
    df_sub <- df %>% filter(Device == dev, Skin.Tone == st,
                            !is.na(HR_GoldStandard),
                            !is.na(HR_Device),
                            HR_GoldStandard >= 30,
                            HR_GoldStandard <= 200)
    res <- fit_inla_diff(df_sub)
    if (!is.null(res)) cbind(Device = dev, Skin.Tone = st, res)
  }))
}))

cat("\n===== TABLA POR SKIN TONE =====\n")
print(tabla_skin)
```

# BRMS

```{r}
library(brms)

get_metrics_brms_long <- function(reloj_name, data) {
  
  df_sub <- data %>%
    filter(Device == reloj_name) %>%
    rename(y = HR_Device, x = HR_GoldStandard) %>%
    filter(!is.na(y), !is.na(x))
  
  if(nrow(df_sub) < 10) return(NULL)
  
  fit <- brm(
    formula = y ~ 1 + x + (1|ID),
    data = df_sub,
    family = gaussian(),
    chains = 4,
    iter = 4000,
    warmup = 2000,
    cores = 4,
    control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95, max_treedepth = 12),
    backend = "rstan",
    silent = 0
  )
  
  draws <- as.data.frame(fit)
  CAD_val <- 5
  
  return(data.frame(
    Reloj = reloj_name,
    CCC = mean((2 * draws$b_x) / (draws$b_x^2 + 1 + (draws$b_Intercept^2 / draws$sigma^2))),
    LoA_Inf = mean(draws$b_Intercept - 1.96 * draws$sigma),
    LoA_Sup = mean(draws$b_Intercept + 1.96 * draws$sigma),
    TDI_95 = as.numeric(quantile(abs(rnorm(nrow(draws), draws$b_Intercept, draws$sigma)), 0.95)),
    CP = mean(abs(rnorm(nrow(draws), draws$b_Intercept, draws$sigma)) <= CAD_val),
    CIA = mean(draws$sd_ID__Intercept^2 / (draws$sd_ID__Intercept^2 + draws$sigma^2))
  ))
}

dispositivos <- unique(df$Device)
tabla_brms <- do.call(rbind, lapply(dispositivos, get_metrics_brms_long, data = df))

print("--- TABLA COMPARATIVA BRMS (DATASET COMPLETO) ---")
print(tabla_brms)
```









#SKIN INLA
```{r}
library(INLA)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

CAD_val <- 5 

# 1. FUNCIÓN MAESTRA TRIPLE (CP Empírico, CP Bayesiano y CCC)
get_all_metrics <- function(reloj_name, tono, data) {
  
  df_sub <- data %>%
    filter(Device == reloj_name, Skin.Tone == tono,
           !is.na(HR_GoldStandard), !is.na(HR_Device),
           as.numeric(HR_GoldStandard) >= 30, as.numeric(HR_GoldStandard) <= 200) %>%
    mutate(
      diff = as.numeric(HR_Device) - as.numeric(HR_GoldStandard),
      ID_idx = as.numeric(as.factor(ID)) 
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(diff))
  
  if(nrow(df_sub) < 30) return(NULL) 
  
  # --- MÉTODO 1: CP EMPÍRICO ---
  cp_empirical <- round(mean(abs(df_sub$diff) <= CAD_val), 4)
  
  # --- MÉTODO 2: CCC (Concordancia Real) ---
  x <- as.numeric(df_sub$HR_GoldStandard)
  y <- as.numeric(df_sub$HR_Device)
  ccc_val <- (2 * cov(x, y)) / (var(x) + var(y) + (mean(x) - mean(y))^2)
  
  # --- MÉTODO 3: CP BAYESIANO (Modelo Jerárquico INLA) ---
  if(length(unique(df_sub$ID_idx)) > 1) {
    fit <- inla(diff ~ 1 + f(ID_idx, model = "iid"), data = df_sub, family = "gaussian")
    alpha <- fit$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "mean"]
    prec_e <- fit$summary.hyperpar["Precision for the Gaussian observations", "mean"]
    prec_id <- fit$summary.hyperpar["Precision for ID_idx", "mean"]
    sigma_tot <- sqrt((1/prec_e) + (1/prec_id))
  } else {
    fit <- inla(diff ~ 1, data = df_sub, family = "gaussian")
    alpha <- fit$summary.fixed["(Intercept)", "mean"]
    prec_e <- fit$summary.hyperpar["Precision for the Gaussian observations", "mean"]
    sigma_tot <- 1 / sqrt(prec_e)
  }
  
  set.seed(123) 
  alpha_sim <- rnorm(20000, alpha, sigma_tot)
  cp_bayesian <- round(mean(abs(alpha_sim) <= CAD_val), 4)
  
  return(data.frame(
    Device = reloj_name, Skin_Tone = as.character(tono), 
    CP_Empirical = cp_empirical, CP_Bayesian = cp_bayesian, CCC = ccc_val
  ))
}

# 2. EJECUCIÓN
tonos_unicos <- unique(df$Skin.Tone)
relojes_unicos <- unique(df$Device)

resultados_lista <- list()
for(r in relojes_unicos) {
  for(t in tonos_unicos) {
    resultados_lista[[paste(r, t)]] <- get_all_metrics(r, t, df)
  }
}

res_skin <- bind_rows(resultados_lista)
res_skin$Skin_Tone <- factor(res_skin$Skin_Tone, levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6"))

# 3. GENERACIÓN DE LOS TRES GRÁFICOS

# Gráfico A: CP Empírico
p1 <- ggplot(res_skin, aes(x = Skin_Tone, y = CP_Empirical, fill = Device)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + 
  labs(title = "CP (Empirical) by Skin Tone", y = "CP Score") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired")

# Gráfico B: CP Bayesiano
p2 <- ggplot(res_skin, aes(x = Skin_Tone, y = CP_Bayesian, fill = Device)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + 
  labs(title = "CP (Bayesian) by Skin Tone", y = "CP Score") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired")

# Gráfico C: CCC (Concordancia)
p3 <- ggplot(res_skin, aes(x = Skin_Tone, y = CCC, fill = Device)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + 
  labs(title = "CCC Score by Skin Tone", y = "CCC Score") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired")

# Mostrar los tres
print(p1)
Sys.sleep(1)
print(p2)
Sys.sleep(1)
print(p3)
```
